profile.name: Patricia
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to lead financial discussions and keep the household on track.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High—comfortable with statements, account types (taxable vs. retirement), and can follow tax and withdrawal sequencing discussions; asks precise follow-up questions.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but preservation-leaning—wants steady income and dislikes surprises; will accept market exposure if the plan shows downside buffers and clear rationale.
profile.communication_style: Direct and organized; often references specific numbers and dates, but will occasionally say 'I might be off by a little' before checking. Uses clarifying questions like 'Are we talking gross or net?' and may gently correct small inaccuracies.
emotional_tendencies: relieved by clear summaries and written next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and checklist-driven; prefers to compare two or three options and decide after confirming assumptions.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting—open to guidance once the advisor demonstrates accuracy, transparency on fees/taxes, and repeats back the facts correctly.
internal_biases: anchoring to current income stream reliability
hidden_concerns: outliving assets and needing a predictable draw plan
tax impact of portfolio income and withdrawals
being a burden if health changes later

profile.name: Danie
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; participates actively but often defers to Patricia on exact figures and paperwork.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate—understands the big picture (income vs. expenses, debt payments, investing basics) but mixes up terminology (balance vs. payment, premium vs. benefit) and prefers plain-English explanations.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate with a comfort-seeking tilt—okay with some risk if it supports lifestyle and simplicity; gets uneasy when discussions become too technical or when outcomes feel uncertain.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and story-based; speaks in round numbers ('about', 'roughly') and uses fillers ('you know', 'I think'). Will ask for quick takeaways and may interrupt with practical questions like 'So what do we do next month?'
emotional_tendencies: stress when confronted with complex paperwork
optimism that things will work out if there is a plan
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; wants Patricia comfortable first, then supports the decision once the advisor frames it simply and ties it to day-to-day impact.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting—assumes competence but needs reassurance through simple explanations and periodic check-ins.
internal_biases: recency bias (reacts to recent market headlines)
hidden_concerns: worry that debt payments are 'dragging' even if balances are small
fear of making an irreversible decision (especially around insurance or big portfolio changes)
